title: The Reconstruction Term
concept: The reconstruction term measures how well the decoder rebuilds the original input from the sampled latent code, just like a normal autoencoder.

exercises.prompt: In a normal autoencoder, what does the decoder try to do?

options.text: Rebuild the original input as closely as possible
Make the input bigger and blurrier

exercises.explain: Autoencoders squeeze data down and then try to rebuild it — the closer the rebuild, the better the model.

cards.title: Can You Rebuild It?
cards.body: Imagine glancing at a face for one second, then trying to redraw it from memory. That's basically what a VAE's decoder does — except instead of your memory, it uses a squeezed-down 'latent code' to try to recreate the original picture!

cards.title: What Is the Reconstruction Term?
cards.body: The reconstruction term is a score that measures how close the decoder's rebuilt output is to the original input. It works by sampling a latent code (a compressed version of the input) and checking: 'Did the decoder turn this back into something that looks like the original?' The smaller the difference, the better the reconstruction.

cards.title: The Sketch Artist
cards.body: Think of a sketch artist who studies a photo, writes down a few quick notes about it (the latent code), then tries to redraw the photo using only those notes. The reconstruction term is like grading that redrawn sketch against the real photo — a great sketch artist gets a high score because the redraw looks just like the original!

cards.title: Faces In, Faces Out
cards.body: A VAE is trained on photos of faces. It compresses a photo into a latent code, then the decoder rebuilds a face from that code. If the rebuilt face has the same skin tone, hairstyle, and expression as the original, the reconstruction term is low (good!). If the rebuilt face looks like a totally different person, the reconstruction term is high (bad).

cards.title: Myth: Reconstruction Is the Only Goal
cards.body: Myth: A VAE only cares about perfect reconstruction, just like a regular autoencoder. Truth: A VAE also has another term (the KL term) that keeps the latent space organized and smooth. Reconstruction alone would let the model memorize codes messily — the KL term keeps things tidy so new, realistic samples can be generated later.

cards.title: Original vs. Rebuilt
cards.body: The reconstruction term compares the input that went in with the output that came out after passing through the encoder and decoder.

left.text: The real photo fed into the encoder

right.text: The decoder's rebuilt version — the closer it matches, the lower the reconstruction loss

exercises.prompt: What does the reconstruction term measure in a VAE?

options.text: How random the latent code is
How well the decoder's output matches the original input
How many layers the network has

exercises.explain: The reconstruction term checks the similarity between the original input and what the decoder rebuilds from the sampled latent code.

exercises.prompt: The reconstruction term works the same basic way it does in a normal (non-variational) autoencoder.
exercises.explain: True! Both compare the rebuilt output to the original input — VAEs just add a sampling step and an extra KL term on top.

exercises.sentence: The reconstruction term is low when the decoder's output closely ___ the original input.
exercises.explain: A low reconstruction term means the rebuilt output closely matches the original — that's the whole point of reconstruction!

exercises.prompt: Which of these would give the WORST (highest) reconstruction term?

options.text: A decoder that rebuilds an almost identical copy of the input
A decoder that rebuilds a slightly blurry but recognizable version of the input
A decoder that rebuilds something completely unrelated to the input

exercises.explain: The more the rebuilt output differs from the original, the worse (higher) the reconstruction term becomes.

exercises.prompt: A VAE decoder is given the latent code for a photo of a dog. It rebuilds an image that looks like a blurry but clearly recognizable dog with the same pose. What does this tell you about the reconstruction term?

options.text: The reconstruction term is high, because the model completely failed
The reconstruction term is low, because the output closely resembles the original input
The reconstruction term doesn't apply here since it's just a dog photo
The reconstruction term only matters for human faces, not animals

exercises.explain: Since the rebuilt image closely resembles the original (same dog, same pose, just a bit blurry), the reconstruction term is low — meaning the decoder did a good job rebuilding the input from the latent code.
